key_insight: An agentic multi-LLM pipeline can autoformalize research-level mathematics in Lean 4, including introducing and checking new definitions when existing libraries are insufficient.
engineering_relevance: This enables mechanically verified translation of mathematical research into proof assistant code, reducing silent reasoning errors and lowering the barrier to formal methods adoption in theory-heavy workflows.
method: Orchestrator coordinates multiple agents for stepwise autoformalization.
Extends missing type definitions and validates them with an Auxiliary Lemma technique before proving the target theorem.
Uses general-purpose coding LLMs rather than Lean-specific fine-tuning.
evidence_signal: The system produced machine-checked Lean proofs for 32 PutnamBench problems and successfully formalized main theorems from five STOC papers, with all five also having proofs formalized and two requiring no axioms beyond Lean's kernel.
watch_item: Performance depends on carefully managing library gaps and auxiliary lemmas, so correctness and robustness may be sensitive to orchestration quality and the availability of human validation for complex cases.
paper_title: Beyond the Library: An Agentic Framework for Autoformalizing Research Mathematics
